_note: Fixture for eval 2 — Feature with a Proposed Tasks checklist spanning distinct backend and frontend layers. The skill must use the checklist as its starting point and keep backend/frontend tasks separate.

feature.title: Feature: Settlement date range filter
feature.body:
## Goal
Let merchants filter their settlement list by a date range so they can focus on a specific billing period without scrolling through all records.

## Bounded Context
Settlement

## User Stories
- As a Merchant, I want to filter settlements by a start and end date so that I can view only the records relevant to a billing period.

## Acceptance Criteria
- AC1: The `GET /settlements` endpoint accepts optional `from` and `to` query parameters (ISO 8601 dates) and returns only settlements whose `settledAt` date falls within the range.
- AC2: Settlement queries filtered by date range use an index on `settledAt` so that response times remain under 200 ms for datasets up to 500,000 rows.
- AC3: The settlement list UI shows a date range picker that lets the merchant select a start and end date; applying the filter reloads the list with the chosen range.
- AC4: The selected date range is persisted in the URL as `?from=YYYY-MM-DD&to=YYYY-MM-DD` so that filtered views are bookmarkable and shareable.

## Edge Cases
- When only `from` is provided the filter returns all settlements on or after that date.
- When only `to` is provided the filter returns all settlements on or before that date.
- Invalid date formats are rejected with a 400 response.

## Domain Events
- None — this feature is read-only.

## Proposed Tasks
- [ ] Add date range filter to settlements API
- [ ] Optimise settlement query for date range lookups
- [ ] Add date range picker component to settlement list UI
- [ ] Wire URL query parameter persistence
